info.title: rfc3339.date — Time API
info.description: A precise, developer-centric time API. Defaults to canonical RFC3339 UTC for /now, supports IANA time zones, strict RFC3339 validation, ISO8601 validation, and conversions across common + exotic representations (NTP, GPS, TAI, JD/MJD, Excel, HTTP-date, RFC5322). Plain text is first-class; JSON is optional via Accept header or ?json=1.

tags.name: Time
tags.description: Current-time endpoints rendered in UTC or a requested zone.

tags.name: Validation
tags.description: Timestamp validation endpoints for single values and batches.

tags.name: Conversion
tags.description: Canonicalization, conversion, and rounding operations across supported encodings.

tags.name: Timezones
tags.description: IANA zone discovery, offset inspection, transition lookup, and human-time conversion.

tags.name: Datasets
tags.description: Versioned supporting datasets used by conversion endpoints.

get.description: Returns the current instant serialized as RFC3339 in UTC (canonical Z). Default response is text/plain.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Fractional second digits to emit (0..9).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Convenience output format alias.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

tz.description: Rendering time zone (UTC or requested IANA)

offset.description: Numeric offset at that instant (e.g. Z, +01:00)

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

get.summary: Current time rendered in an IANA time zone
get.description: Returns the current instant rendered in the requested IANA time zone, including numeric offset. Same instant as /now, different representation.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: IANA time zone ID (URL-encoded), e.g. Europe%2FBerlin

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Fractional second digits to emit (0..9).

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Convenience output format alias.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

200.description: RFC3339 timestamp in requested zone (includes offset)

tz.description: Rendering time zone (UTC or requested IANA)

offset.description: Numeric offset at that instant (e.g. Z, +01:00)

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)

get.description: Validates the given value against a specified profile. In strict mode, rejects non-canonical/grammar-violating inputs. In lenient mode, may accept parseable inputs with warnings and provide canonical output when possible.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Input timestamp string to validate.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Validation profile.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Strict rejects non-compliant inputs; lenient may accept with warnings.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

fraction.description: Fractional seconds as digits (no dot), up to 9.

schema.description: If Accept=text/plain (or ?json=0), returns either "VALID" or "INVALID" plus optional single-line reason.

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

fraction.description: Fractional seconds as digits (no dot), up to 9.

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

get.description: Parses the given value under the selected profile and returns canonical RFC3339 UTC by default. If out is set, returns canonical output for that target format.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: Validation profile.

parameters.name: out
parameters.description: Output encoding.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Fractional second digits to emit (0..9).

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: IANA time zone for rendering outputs that are zone-dependent.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)

get.description: Converts an input value from an input encoding to an output encoding. If in=auto, ambiguity results in 400 with candidates. For timezone-dependent output formats, supply tz=IANA. For standards requiring leap-second tables (TAI/GPS), supply leapdata=latest or a known version.

parameters.name: in
parameters.description: Input encoding.

parameters.name: out
parameters.description: Output encoding.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Fractional second digits to emit (0..9).

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: IANA time zone for rendering outputs that are zone-dependent.

parameters.name: leapdata
parameters.description: Leap-second dataset version identifier, or "latest".

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)

get.summary: Round a timestamp to a unit

parameters.name: in
parameters.description: Input encoding.

parameters.name: out
parameters.description: Output encoding.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Fractional second digits to emit (0..9).

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: IANA time zone for rendering outputs that are zone-dependent.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

get.description: Returns a list of IANA time zone IDs matching q (substring match). If q is omitted, returns a curated list (or an implementation-defined subset).

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

get.summary: Convert a human-style local time into another IANA time zone
get.description: Parses human-oriented local times such as `5pm DST`, `tomorrow 3am CET`, or `2026-05-22 17:35 CEST`, resolves them to an instant, and renders that instant in the target IANA time zone. Supported source/target zone tokens include IANA zones, `UTC`, numeric offsets, and a curated abbreviation set (for example CET/CEST/EST/EDT/PST/PDT). `DST` and `STD` require `from=<IANA zone>` so the worker can resolve the regional rules. Plain text returns the target local RFC3339 value; JSON adds parsed source/target details.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Human-style local time. Examples: `5pm DST`, `tomorrow 3am CET`, `2026-05-22 17:35 CEST`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Target zone token. Accepts IANA zones, `UTC`, curated abbreviations, or numeric offsets.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Source IANA time zone used when the value omits a zone or uses DST/STD or an abbreviation hint that should be validated against a real region.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: RFC3339 base instant used to resolve relative values such as `tomorrow` and time-only inputs.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Fractional second digits to emit in rendered source/target values.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

200.description: Converted target local time plus optional source/target metadata

base.description: RFC3339 instant used for resolving relative or time-only inputs.

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)

get.summary: Offset/DST information for a zone at a given instant

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: IANA time zone ID (URL-encoded), e.g. Europe%2FBerlin

parameters.name: at
parameters.description: RFC3339 instant to evaluate; default is now.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: If 1, forces JSON output regardless of Accept header.

abbreviation.description: Best-effort (may be null; abbreviations are not stable/portable).

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)

get.summary: DST/offset transitions for a zone in a time range
get.description: Returns boundaries where the numeric offset changes within the requested range. Implementation can compute transitions by scanning offsets and refining boundary instants.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: RFC3339 instant (inclusive)

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: RFC3339 instant (exclusive)

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Scan step used to detect transitions before boundary refinement.

parameters.name: refine
parameters.description: Refinement resolution for transition instant.

at.description: RFC3339 instant where the new offset takes effect (best-effort resolution)

400.description: Bad request (invalid input, ambiguity, or unsupported combination)

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)

get.summary: Leap second dataset + current TAI-UTC offset
get.description: Provides leap second table used for UTC<->TAI and GPS conversions. Versioning allows reproducible results.

taiMinusUtc.description: Current TAI-UTC seconds offset per dataset.

source.description: Dataset provenance (e.g., IERS bulletin identifier and publication date).

effective.description: RFC3339 date-time at which the offset becomes effective (UTC).

404.description: Not found (unknown time zone or resource)
